text.title: Load Balancer Health Checks Procedure
text.summary: 5-step procedure to apply the lb-health-checks methodology end-to-end.

step.name: enumerate-dependencies
step.action: List every external system the request path needs (DB, cache, broker, downstream HTTP). Mark each as critical (required for any traffic) or optional (degrade gracefully). Pick a probe timeout per critical dep (default 3 s).

step.name: implement-handlers
step.action: Implement `/health/live` returning 200 unconditionally. Implement `/health/ready` running each critical dep check in parallel, each wrapped in its timeout. Return 200 only if all critical checks pass; 503 otherwise with a JSON map showing which dep failed.

step.name: wire-lb-probes
step.action: Kubernetes: pod spec with `livenessProbe` → /health/live + `readinessProbe` → /health/ready + `startupProbe` for slow boots. HAProxy: `option httpchk GET /health/ready` + `http-check expect status 200`. Nginx Plus: `health_check uri=/health/ready match=ok`. Cloud LB target group: HTTP /health/ready, healthy_threshold 2, unhealthy_threshold 3.

step.name: tune-thresholds
step.input: SLO for failure detection + flapping budget.
step.action: Default interval 10 s, healthy 2, unhealthy 3. For latency-critical services tighten unhealthy to 2 (faster eviction at cost of more flap risk). For long boots (DB migrations) add `startupProbe` with `failureThreshold: 30`.

step.name: validate-and-chaos-test
step.action: Run `validate-lb-health-checks.py --file artefact.json`. Chaos test: stop the DB; confirm `/health/ready` returns 503 within (timeout + interval) seconds; confirm LB removes the backend within (interval × unhealthy_threshold) seconds.
step.decision-gate: If chaos test does not remove the pod within budget, tighten thresholds before going live.
